heading: Leongatha South vs Lerderderg — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Leongatha South, Victoria and Lerderderg, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Leongatha South has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Lerderderg (32/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Leongatha South or Lerderderg safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Leongatha South has a flood risk score of 24/100, while Lerderderg scores 49/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Leongatha South scores 18/100 for bushfire risk. Lerderderg scores 20/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Leongatha South or Lerderderg?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Leongatha South has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.27% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
